We will use abelian quotients.

Theorem 41 
                            
                               Let  
                               
                                  G 
                                
                               be a totally ordered group and  
                               
                                  H 
                                
                               be a nontrivial normal convex subgroup. If  
                               
                                  G 
                                  ∕ 
                                  H 
                                
                               is abelian, then  
                               
                                  G 
                                
                               has a normal final segment  
                               
                                  F 
                                
                               such that  
                               
                                  I 
                                  ≔ 
                                  G 
                                  ∖ 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     − 
                                     F 
                                     ∪ 
                                     F 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                
                               is not a subgroup of  
                               
                                  G 
                               
                               .

Let  
                               
                                  G 
                                
                               be a totally ordered group and  
                               
                                  H 
                                
                               be a nontrivial normal convex subgroup. If  
                               
                                  G 
                                  ∕ 
                                  H 
                                
                               is abelian, then  
                               
                                  G 
                                
                               has a normal final segment  
                               
                                  F 
                                
                               such that  
                               
                                  I 
                                  ≔ 
                                  G 
                                  ∖ 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     − 
                                     F 
                                     ∪ 
                                     F 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                
                               is not a subgroup of  
                               
                                  G 
                               
                               .

Proof 
                            Let  
                                  α 
                                  ∈ 
                                  G 
                                be such that  
                                  0 
                                  < 
                                  α 
                                and  
                                  α 
                                  ⁄ 
                                  ∈ 
                                  H 
                                and set  
                                  
                                     
                                        F 
                                        ≔ 
                                        
                                           { 
                                           g 
                                           ∈ 
                                           G 
                                           : 
                                           g 
                                           + 
                                           H 
                                           > 
                                           α 
                                           + 
                                           H 
                                           } 
                                        
                                        . 
                                     
                                  
                               
                            
                            
                               
                                  
                                     • 
                                     
                                        
                                           F 
                                           ≠ 
                                           ∅ 
                                        . Indeed, since  
                                           I 
                                         is nontrivial  
                                           G 
                                           ∕ 
                                           H 
                                        , which is totally ordered, must be infinite. 
                                  
                                  
                                     • 
                                     
                                        
                                           α 
                                           ⁄ 
                                           ∈ 
                                           F 
                                        . 
                                  
                                  
                                     • 
                                     Every element of  
                                           F 
                                         is positive. Let  
                                           g 
                                           ≤ 
                                           0 
                                        . Then  
                                           g 
                                           + 
                                           H 
                                           ≤ 
                                           0 
                                           + 
                                           H 
                                           < 
                                           α 
                                           + 
                                           H 
                                         and hence  
                                           g 
                                           ⁄ 
                                           ∈ 
                                           F 
                                        . 
                                  
                                  
                                     • 
                                     
                                        
                                           F 
                                         is a final segment of  
                                           G 
                                        . Let  
                                           f 
                                           , 
                                           g 
                                           ∈ 
                                           G 
                                         be such that  
                                           f 
                                           ∈ 
                                           F 
                                         and  
                                           f 
                                           ≤ 
                                           g 
                                        . Then  
                                           α 
                                           + 
                                           H 
                                           < 
                                           f 
                                           + 
                                           H 
                                           ≤ 
                                           g 
                                           + 
                                           H 
                                        . Hence,  
                                           g 
                                           ∈ 
                                           F 
                                        . 
                                  
                                  
                                     • 
                                     
                                        
                                           F 
                                         is normal. Let  
                                           f 
                                           ∈ 
                                           F 
                                         and  
                                           g 
                                           ∈ 
                                           G 
                                        . Then  
                                           
                                              
                                                 
                                                    g 
                                                    + 
                                                    f 
                                                    − 
                                                    g 
                                                    + 
                                                    H 
                                                    = 
                                                    
                                                       ( 
                                                       g 
                                                       + 
                                                       H 
                                                       ) 
                                                    
                                                    + 
                                                    
                                                       ( 
                                                       f 
                                                       + 
                                                       H 
                                                       ) 
                                                    
                                                    − 
                                                    
                                                       ( 
                                                       g 
                                                       + 
                                                       H 
                                                       ) 
                                                    
                                                    = 
                                                    
                                                       ( 
                                                       g 
                                                       + 
                                                       H 
                                                       ) 
                                                    
                                                    − 
                                                    
                                                       ( 
                                                       g 
                                                       + 
                                                       H 
                                                       ) 
                                                    
                                                    + 
                                                    
                                                       ( 
                                                       f 
                                                       + 
                                                       H 
                                                       ) 
                                                    
                                                     (this is because  
                                                    G 
                                                    ∕ 
                                                    H 
                                                     is abelian) 
                                                    = 
                                                    f 
                                                    + 
                                                    H 
                                                    > 
                                                    α 
                                                    + 
                                                    H 
                                                    . 
                                                 
                                              
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                  
                                  
                                     • 
                                     
                                        
                                           I 
                                         is not a subgroup of  
                                           G 
                                        . Note that since  
                                           α 
                                           ⁄ 
                                           ∈ 
                                           F 
                                         and  
                                           0 
                                           < 
                                           α 
                                         we infer that  
                                           α 
                                           ∈ 
                                           I 
                                         (in particular  
                                           I 
                                           ≠ 
                                           H 
                                        ). Since  
                                           2 
                                           α 
                                           − 
                                           α 
                                           = 
                                           α 
                                           ⁄ 
                                           ∈ 
                                           H

                                           2 
                                           α 
                                           + 
                                           H 
                                           ≠ 
                                           α 
                                           + 
                                           H

                                           α

                                           α

                                           α

                                           2

                                           F

                                           2 
                                           α 
                                           ⁄ 
                                           ∈ 
                                           I 
                                         proving that  
                                           I 
                                         is not a subgroup of  
                                           G 
                                        . □

                                           = 
                                           α 
                                           ⁄ 
                                           ∈ 
                                           H 
                                         we infer that  
                                           2 
                                           α 
                                           + 
                                           H 
                                           ≠ 
                                           α 
                                           + 
                                           H 
                                        . From  
                                           α 
                                           < 
                                           2 
                                           α

                                           α

                                           H

                                           α

                                           H

                                           2

                                           F 
                                        . Hence,  
                                           2 
                                           α 
                                           ⁄ 
                                           ∈ 
                                           I

                                           I

                                           G 
                                        . □

F 
                                           ≠ 
                                           ∅

                                           I

                                           G

                                           H

                                           ⁄

We now consider finitely generated groups.

Theorem 42 
                            
                               Let  
                               
                                  G 
                                
                               be a finitely generated nonarchimedean totally ordered group. Then 
                               
                                  G 
                                
                               has a nontrivial normal convex subgroup  
                               
                                  H 
                                
                               such that  
                               
                                  G 
                                  ∕ 
                                  H 
                                
                               is abelian.

We now proceed to the proof of  Theorem 13 . Let  
                            G 
                          be a totally ordered group. If  
                            G 
                          is archimedean, then it follows from Hölder’s Theorem that  
                            G 
                          is abelian. From  Corollary 12  we deduce that  
                            G 
                          has a nonempty final segment  
                            F 
                          verifying  
                            
                               ( 
                               b 
                               ) 
                            
                          of  Corollary 12  hence  
                            H 
                          is not a subgroup. On the other hand, if  
                            G 
                          is nonarchimedean and finitely generated, then the required conclusion follows from  Theorems 41 and 42 .

Let  
                         G 
                       be a threshold group, with attained threshold  
                         α 
                      . Our aim is to prove in an effective way that the dimension of the order is at most  
                         3 
                      .

                            A threshold group

                            .

Remark 1 
                         None of the linear orders  
                               
                                  
                                     ≤ 
                                  
                                  
                                     i 
                                  
                               
                            , for  
                               i 
                               = 
                               1 
                               , 
                               2

                               3 
                            , is compatible with the group operation (still  
                               
                                  
                                     ≤

                             is preserved by the translation  
                               
                                  
                                     t

                            . There are very rare cases for which the order of an ordered group is the intersection of compatible linear orders. In fact, in many cases, there is just one compatible linear order extending the order. For an example, this is the case for the threshold order  
                               
                                  
                                     ≤ 
                                  
                                  
                                     1 
                                  
                               
                             on  
                               R 
                            .

                            . There are very rare cases for which the order of an ordered group is the intersection of compatible linear orders. In fact, in many cases, there is just one compatible linear order extending the order. For an example, this is the case for the threshold order

We introduce the following notion. Let  
                         P 
                         , 
                         Q 
                       be two posets, we set  
                         P 
                         ⪯ 
                         Q 
                       if  
                         P 
                       can be embedded in every ordered group  
                         G 
                       in which  
                         Q 
                       can be embedded. This defines a quasi-order on the class of posets. This quasi-order extends the embeddability relation, that is if  
                         P 
                       embeds in  
                         Q 
                      , then  
                         P 
                         ⪯ 
                         Q 
                      . The equivalence relation corresponding to this quasi-order is defined by  
                         P 
                         ≃ 
                         Q 
                       if  
                         P 
                         ⪯ 
                         Q 
                       and  
                         Q 
                         ⪯ 
                         P 
                      . We set  
                         P 
                         ≺ 
                         Q 
                       if  
                         P 
                         ⪯ 
                         Q 
                       and  
                         Q 
                         ⁄ 
                         ⪯ 
                         P 
                      . Observe that if  
                         P 
                       is a 1-element chain, the equivalence class of  
                         P 
                       consists of the 1-element chains. Hence, if we consider posets up to isomorphism, this class has just one element. Similarly:

( 
                                  α 
                               ) 
                            
                               If  
                               
                                  P 
                                
                               is a  
                               
                                  n 
                               
                               -element antichain, the equivalence class consists of “the”  
                               
                                  n 
                               
                               -element antichain . 
                            Indeed, we claim that if  
                                  Q 
                                  ≺ 
                                  P 
                                then  
                                  Q 
                                is an antichain of size at most  
                                  n 
                                  − 
                                  1 
                               . For that, let  
                                  G 
                                be the direct product  
                                  G 
                                  : 
                                  = 
                                  Z 
                                  ×

                                     Z

                                     n 
                                     Z

                               . Select as positive cone the set  
                                  
                                     
                                        C 
                                     
                                     
                                        1 
                                     
                                  
                                  ≔

                                        ( 
                                        m 
                                        , 
                                        0 
                                        )

                                     m 
                                     ≥ 
                                     0 
                                     } 
                                  
                               . The resulting ordered group is a direct sum of  
                                  n 
                                copies of  
                                  Z

                                  P

                                  Q

                                  Q 
                                is a direct sum of chains. Select as positive cone  
                                  
                                     
                                        C 
                                     
                                     
                                        2 
                                     
                                  
                                  ≔

                               . The order is a lexicographical sum over  
                                  Z

                                  n 
                               -element antichains. Again, this poset embeds  
                                  P

                                  Q

                                  Q 
                                is an antichain and since it cannot be isomorphic to  
                                  P 
                                it has less than  
                                  n 
                                elements proving our claim.

                                  0

                                  Z

                                  G

                                  Z 
                               .

Proof 
                         (1). As we have seen with  Theorem 4 , all posets of the form  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  q 
                                  + 
                                  1 
                                  ) 
                               
                               ⊕ 
                               p 
                             for positive  
                               p 
                             and  
                               q 
                             such that  
                               p 
                               + 
                               q 
                               = 
                               n 
                             are equivalent. To see that the equivalence class contains nothing else, let  
                               Q 
                               ⪯ 
                               P 
                             with  
                               P 
                               ∈ 
                               
                                  
                                     S 
                                  
                                  
                                     n 
                                  
                               
                            . According to ( 
                               γ 
                            ),  
                               Q 
                             is a direct sum of two chains. Let  
                               G 
                             be the group  
                               Z 
                             with positive cone  
                               2 
                               N 
                               ∪ 
                               
                                  ( 
                                  n 
                                  + 
                                  1 
                                  ) 
                               
                               N 
                             if  
                               n 
                             is even and equal to  
                               Z 
                               × 
                               Z 
                               ∕ 
                               2 
                               Z 
                             with strict positive cone generated by  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  1 
                                  , 
                                  0 
                                  ) 
                               
                             and  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  p 
                                  + 
                                  1 
                                  , 
                                  1 
                                  ) 
                               
                             if  
                               n 
                             is odd,  
                               n 
                               = 
                               2 
                               p 
                               + 
                               1 
                            . This group contains  
                               1 
                               ⊕ 
                               n 
                             but not  
                               1 
                               ⊕ 
                               
                                  ( 
                                  n 
                                  + 
                                  1 
                                  ) 
                               
                            . Hence, according to  Theorem 4 , it contains  
                               P 
                             and hence  
                               Q 
                            . If  
                               Q 
                             is a chain then  
                               Q 
                             is equivalent to the  
                               1 
                            - element or the  
                               2 
                            -element chain, and thus  
                               Q 
                               ≺ 
                               P 
                            . If  
                               Q 
                             is not a chain, then via  Theorem 4 , the cardinality of  
                               Q 
                             is at most  
                               n 
                               + 
                               1 
                            . If  
                               
                                  | 
                                  Q 
                                  | 
                               
                               < 
                               
                                  | 
                                  P 
                                  | 
                               
                            , then  
                               Q 
                               ≺ 
                               P 
                            , whereas, if  
                               
                                  | 
                                  Q 
                                  | 
                               
                               =
